home.title: Arké Tarot
home.subtitle: Draw cards. Listen to what arises.

oneCard.name: One Card Pull
oneCard.description: A single card for daily guidance, a yes/no question, or a simple focus.

positions.cardDesc: Your guidance, focus, or answer for today. Sit with what first catches your attention — that initial impression is often the truest message. Let the card accompany you through the day as a point of quiet reflection.

twoCard.name: Two Card
twoCard.description: Two options, or cause and effect. Use when weighing a choice or exploring a dynamic.

positions.optionADesc: The first path, option, or contributing cause. This card reveals the energy, nature, and likely consequences of this direction or force. Consider what draws you here and what it might ask of you.
positions.optionBDesc: The second path, option, or resulting effect. This card shows what arises from or contrasts with the first. The tension or harmony between the two cards together often holds the real insight.

threeCardPPF.name: Three Card — Past / Present / Future
threeCardPPF.description: Reveals the timeline of a situation: what shaped it, where it stands, and where it is heading.

positions.pastDesc: What has led to the current situation. This card reflects the events, decisions, or energies that shaped where you are now — still close enough to feel. Notice what it brought before releasing it.
positions.presentDesc: The current state or energy. This card captures what is most alive in your life right now: the dominant force, challenge, or opportunity you are actively navigating. Meet it honestly.
positions.futureDesc: The direction things are heading. This card shows a likely outcome or incoming energy based on current momentum. It is not fixed fate — use it as guidance for choices you still hold.

threeCardMBS.name: Three Card — Mind / Body / Spirit
threeCardMBS.description: A holistic snapshot of your wellbeing across mental, physical, and spiritual dimensions.

positions.mindDesc: Your thoughts, beliefs, and mental state. This card reflects what you are telling yourself, the stories running in the background, and the quality of your attention. Notice what you keep thinking about.
positions.bodyDesc: Physical health, energy, and material reality. This card speaks to your body, home, finances, and relationship to the physical world. Pay attention to where you feel it literally in your body.
positions.spiritDesc: Your spiritual state, intuition, and higher self. This card points to what your soul is asking for — the deeper current beneath daily life. It often speaks through feelings, symbols, and quiet knowing rather than logic.

threeCardSAO.name: Three Card — Situation / Action / Outcome
threeCardSAO.description: Diagnoses a situation, suggests an action, and shows a likely outcome.

positions.situationDesc: The current circumstances or challenge. This card describes the landscape you are in: the forces at play, the context, and what is objectively present. See it clearly before deciding how to move.
positions.actionDesc: The recommended action or approach. This card suggests how to engage: what energy to bring, what move to make, or what inner stance will serve you best. It points toward the most aligned response.
positions.outcomeDesc: The likely result if you follow this path. This card shows what emerges when you take the suggested action. It is conditional — it shifts as your choices and inner state shift.

triad.name: The Triad
triad.description: A dialectical spread: thesis, antithesis, synthesis. Ideal for resolving tensions.

positions.thesisDesc: The initial position, assertion, or force. This card represents what is being held, asserted, or enacted — the starting point of a tension and the energy you have been operating from.
positions.antithesisDesc: The opposing force, counter-position, or challenge. This card reveals what pushes back or is being resisted. It demands acknowledgment rather than dismissal — its voice contains something essential.
positions.synthesisDesc: The resolution, integration, or higher truth. This card shows what becomes possible when both forces are truly heard. It often points beyond the original conflict to something genuinely new.

celticCross.name: Celtic Cross
celticCross.description: The classic 10-card spread offering a comprehensive view of a situation: its roots, influences, and potential outcomes.

positions.presentDesc: The heart of the matter; the current situation. This is the central card of the entire reading — it names the essence of what is being examined and the energy most alive in your life right now. Everything else in the spread relates back to this card.
positions.challengeDesc: What crosses or challenges the present energy. Laid across the central card, this represents the immediate obstacle, complicating force, or dynamic in direct tension with the core situation. It can act as hindrance or unexpected support.
positions.pastDesc: Recent past influences leading to now. This card reflects what has just moved through — events, energies, or decisions that shaped the current moment and are still close enough to cast a shadow or lend momentum.
positions.futureDesc: The near future; where things are moving. This card shows what is coming into being in the short term — a likely next step, an incoming energy, or the direction the situation is naturally flowing toward.
positions.aboveDesc: Conscious goal or best possible outcome. This card reflects what you are consciously striving toward, hoping for, or what the situation could become at its highest potential. It represents your aspiration or ideal outcome.
positions.belowDesc: Unconscious influences or foundation. This card points to what lies beneath the surface: root causes, buried desires, deep fears, or the hidden ground the entire situation rests upon. It is often the most revealing card in the spread.
positions.attitudeDesc: Your attitude, fears, or self-perception. This is how you see yourself in this situation — your inner stance, the story you are carrying about who you are, and how you are showing up. It reveals the lens you are looking through.
positions.externalDesc: Environmental influences and others' perceptions. This card reveals what is coming from outside — how others see you or the situation, and what external forces are shaping events beyond your direct control.
positions.hopesDesc: Your hopes or fears about the outcome. This is a paradoxical position: it can represent what you most desire or what you most dread — often both at once, or one masking the other just beneath the surface.
positions.outcomeDesc: The final outcome if the current path continues. This culminating card integrates all the others into a likely resolution. It shows where things are heading if the energies present are allowed to unfold naturally.

horseshoe.name: Horseshoe Spread
horseshoe.description: A seven-card arc offering a concise yet complete view: what was, what is, what is hidden, what blocks you, how others see it, what to do, and where it leads.

positions.pastDesc: What has shaped the current situation. This card reflects the recent past — the events, choices, or energies that set the current situation in motion. It is still close enough to exert influence; understanding it helps you see how you arrived here.
positions.presentDesc: The current state of the matter. This card captures the dominant energy alive in the situation right now — the force most actively at work. Meet it honestly before deciding how to respond.
positions.hiddenDesc: Unseen forces shaping the situation from below. This card points to what is operating beneath the surface — unconscious patterns, undisclosed factors, or energies not yet visible to the people involved. Bringing it into awareness is itself a form of action.
positions.obstaclesDesc: What stands in the way. This card names the central challenge or block — the friction point between where you are and where you want to be. Seeing it clearly is the first step to moving through or around it.
positions.attitudesDesc: How others perceive or are affecting the situation. This card reveals the external perspective — how those around you see the matter, what energy they bring, and how their attitudes are influencing what unfolds.
positions.actionDesc: The recommended course of action. This card suggests how to move forward: what energy to bring, what inner stance to adopt, or what concrete step will most align you with the best possible outcome.
positions.outcomeDesc: The likely result if the current path continues. This culminating card shows where things are heading based on the energies at play. It is not fixed — but it is the most probable conclusion given all that the other six cards have revealed.

zodiac.name: Astrological / Zodiac Spread
zodiac.description: Twelve cards, one for each house of the zodiac, offering insight across all life areas.

positions.house1Desc: Identity, appearance, new beginnings. This card speaks to how you are showing up in the world right now: your sense of self, your physical presence, and what is just beginning in your personal story.
positions.house2Desc: Money, possessions, values. This card reflects your relationship to what you own, earn, and consider worth protecting — including what you value most deeply, not just materially.
positions.house3Desc: Siblings, short travel, learning. This card reflects your close relationships, daily communication, and how you gather and share information. It may also speak to nearby journeys or studies currently underway.
positions.house4Desc: Family, roots, private life. This card speaks to the home, ancestry, emotional foundations, and what you return to for security. It reflects both your past and the place you go to restore yourself.
positions.house5Desc: Romance, children, pleasure. This card reflects joy, creativity, play, and romantic expression. It asks: where is your delight right now, and are you giving yourself permission to live it?
positions.house6Desc: Work, health, daily routine. This card speaks to how you tend your body and your responsibilities — the daily rhythms and habits that either sustain your energy or quietly deplete it.
positions.house7Desc: Relationships, marriage, open enemies. This card reflects your one-on-one partnerships — romantic, business, or otherwise — and what you are drawing to yourself or projecting onto others.
positions.house8Desc: Death, rebirth, shared resources, the occult. This card speaks to transformation, what must be released, deep intimacy, and the hidden forces that move beneath the visible surface of your life.
positions.house9Desc: Higher learning, travel, beliefs. This card reflects your philosophical outlook, spiritual search, long journeys, and the quest to expand your understanding of what life means and where it is taking you.
positions.house10Desc: Public status, vocation, ambition. This card speaks to your career, reputation, and the mark you are making on the world. It reflects where you stand publicly and what you are building toward.
positions.house11Desc: Friends, groups, goals, social causes. This card reflects your community, long-term aspirations, and the collective endeavors that inspire you. It asks who you are when you belong to something larger than yourself.
positions.house12Desc: Secrets, karma, spirituality, self-undoing. This card speaks to what is hidden, unconscious, or carried from past cycles. It points to where retreat, solitude, and deep inner work are most needed right now.

yearlyCalendar.name: Yearly Calendar Spread
yearlyCalendar.description: Thirteen cards: one for each month of the year plus a central theme card for the year as a whole.

positions.month1Desc: Energy and theme for January. This card holds the dominant focus, lesson, or invitation for this month. Return to it at the month's start as an orientation and at its end as a reflection on what unfolded.
positions.month2Desc: Energy and theme for February. This card holds the dominant focus, lesson, or invitation for this month. Return to it at the month's start as an orientation and at its end as a reflection on what unfolded.
positions.month3Desc: Energy and theme for March. This card holds the dominant focus, lesson, or invitation for this month. Return to it at the month's start as an orientation and at its end as a reflection on what unfolded.
positions.month4Desc: Energy and theme for April. This card holds the dominant focus, lesson, or invitation for this month. Return to it at the month's start as an orientation and at its end as a reflection on what unfolded.
positions.month5Desc: Energy and theme for May. This card holds the dominant focus, lesson, or invitation for this month. Return to it at the month's start as an orientation and at its end as a reflection on what unfolded.
positions.month6Desc: Energy and theme for June. This card holds the dominant focus, lesson, or invitation for this month. Return to it at the month's start as an orientation and at its end as a reflection on what unfolded.
positions.month7Desc: Energy and theme for July. This card holds the dominant focus, lesson, or invitation for this month. Return to it at the month's start as an orientation and at its end as a reflection on what unfolded.
positions.month8Desc: Energy and theme for August. This card holds the dominant focus, lesson, or invitation for this month. Return to it at the month's start as an orientation and at its end as a reflection on what unfolded.
positions.month9Desc: Energy and theme for September. This card holds the dominant focus, lesson, or invitation for this month. Return to it at the month's start as an orientation and at its end as a reflection on what unfolded.
positions.month10Desc: Energy and theme for October. This card holds the dominant focus, lesson, or invitation for this month. Return to it at the month's start as an orientation and at its end as a reflection on what unfolded.
positions.month11Desc: Energy and theme for November. This card holds the dominant focus, lesson, or invitation for this month. Return to it at the month's start as an orientation and at its end as a reflection on what unfolded.
positions.month12Desc: Energy and theme for December. This card holds the dominant focus, lesson, or invitation for this month. Return to it at the month's start as an orientation and at its end as a reflection on what unfolded.
positions.themeDesc: The overarching energy and lesson of the entire year. This central card acts as the soul of the reading — the thread running through all twelve months and the deeper invitation this year holds for you.

birthdaySolar.name: Birthday / Solar Return Spread
birthdaySolar.description: An annual personal forecast for the year ahead, drawn on or near your birthday.

positions.yearThemeDesc: The overall energy and lesson of your new year. This card sets the tone for everything ahead — the central invitation, the quality of energy you will be working with, and the deeper purpose this cycle holds.
positions.innerSelfDesc: Your internal world, feelings, and subconscious. This card reveals what is alive beneath the surface: the emotional undercurrent, the inner work calling for attention, the self that only you know.
positions.focusDesc: What to direct your attention and energy toward. This card points to the area or quality most deserving of your investment this year — where showing up fully will yield the most meaningful return.
positions.outerWorldDesc: How you appear to others and the external environment. This card reflects the face you are presenting to the world and what is moving in your outer life: your circumstances, relationships, and public presence.
positions.challengeDesc: The main challenge or obstacle you will face. This card names a difficulty that is not here to defeat you but to develop you. Understanding it in advance lets you meet it with greater awareness and choice.
positions.opportunityDesc: The main opportunity or gift available to you. This card points to an opening — an area where, if you step forward with intention, something meaningful and lasting can emerge in the year ahead.
positions.supportDesc: What or who will support you through the year. This card reveals the resource, relationship, quality, or practice that will be your most reliable anchor and ally when things get difficult.
positions.lessonDesc: The key spiritual or personal lesson of this year. This card names the theme your soul is working to understand and integrate — wisdom you will carry forward long after this year has passed.
positions.giftDesc: The gift you are being given or will develop. This card points to something that will emerge through this year's experiences: a capacity, a clarity, or a quality you will come to own more fully.

constellation.name: Constellation Spread
constellation.description: A free-form spread where you place cards intuitively on an open surface. Assign roles and positions as feels right, then interpret based on where each card lands.

notes.label: Your Consultation and Reflections
notes.placeholder: Write about your consultation, thoughts, feelings, and reflections on this reading...

ai.error: The connection failed. Please try again.
ai.configure: Configure an AI provider in Settings to get detailed interpretations.

copyPrompt.title: Copy Reading Prompt
copyPrompt.description: Paste this into any AI assistant to get an interpretation.
copyPrompt.reflectionsPlaceholder: Write about your consultation, thoughts, feelings, and reflections...

constellation.instructions: Click "Add Card" to draw and label each card, then drag to position it.

labelDialog.title: Name This Card
labelDialog.prompt: What role does this card play in your reading?
labelDialog.placeholder: e.g. What I need to release

log.title: Reading Log
log.emptyHint: Complete a reading and save it to see it here.
log.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this reading? This cannot be undone.

reversal.label: Allow Reversed Cards
reversal.chanceHint: Probability that a drawn card is reversed (0 = never, 1 = always)

ai.label: AI Interpretation
ai.apiKeyHint: Your key is stored locally and never sent to our servers.

ar00.name: The Fool
ar00.upright: New beginnings, innocence, spontaneity, free spirit, adventure
ar00.description: The Fool stands at the edge of a cliff, pack in hand, about to step into the unknown. He represents pure potential — the soul at the start of its journey, unencumbered by expectation or fear. The Fool embodies the courage to begin without knowing how things will unfold.

ar01.name: The Magician
ar01.reversed: Manipulation, poor planning, untapped talent, deception
ar01.description: The Magician stands before a table bearing all four suits — wand, cup, sword, and pentacle. Above his head hangs the lemniscate (infinity symbol). He has all the tools at his disposal and the will to use them. The Magician represents the conscious mind directing energy into manifestation.

ar02.name: The High Priestess
ar02.upright: Intuition, mystery, the subconscious, inner knowledge, divine feminine
ar02.reversed: Secrets, withdrawal, repressed intuition, hidden agendas
ar02.description: The High Priestess sits between two pillars — B (Boaz) and J (Jachin) — at the threshold of a temple. A crescent moon rests at her feet; a pomegranate veil behind her hides the mysteries. She is the keeper of hidden knowledge and asks us to look inward rather than outward for answers.

ar03.name: The Empress
ar03.upright: Abundance, nurturing, fertility, nature, creativity, feminine power
ar03.reversed: Dependence, smothering, emptiness, creative block, neglect
ar03.description: The Empress sits on a lush throne surrounded by wheat, forest, and flowing water. She wears a crown of twelve stars and holds a scepter. She represents the generative power of nature, the nurturing archetype, and the creative life force that sustains all living things.

ar04.name: The Emperor
ar04.upright: Authority, structure, stability, leadership, discipline, fatherhood
ar04.reversed: Domination, rigidity, inflexibility, loss of control, excessive control
ar04.description: The Emperor sits on a solid stone throne adorned with rams' heads, symbolizing Aries. He rules through reason, will, and the establishment of order. Where the Empress is abundance and flow, the Emperor is structure and boundary — the archetype of worldly authority and paternal stability.

ar05.name: The Hierophant
ar05.upright: Tradition, conformity, institutions, spiritual guidance, shared beliefs
ar05.reversed: Rebellion, subversiveness, challenging the status quo, personal belief
ar05.description: The Hierophant sits between two pillars and holds a triple scepter, making a blessing gesture. Two acolytes kneel before him. He represents established institutions, organized religion, and the transmission of cultural wisdom from generation to generation.

ar06.name: The Lovers
ar06.upright: Love, union, alignment, relationships, choices, values
ar06.reversed: Disharmony, imbalance, misalignment of values, difficult choices
ar06.description: A man and woman stand beneath an angel (Raphael, angel of air). Behind the man is the tree of knowledge bearing serpent and fruit; behind the woman is a tree of flames. The Lovers represent not only romantic love but the deeper union of opposites and the necessity of conscious choice.

ar07.name: The Chariot
ar07.upright: Determination, willpower, victory, control, direction, triumph
ar07.reversed: Aggression, lack of control, directionless, scattered energy
ar07.description: A warrior rides a chariot pulled by two sphinxes — one black, one white — symbolizing opposing forces held in check by will. He wears armor decorated with crescent moons and holds no reins, directing the sphinxes through focused intention. The Chariot represents disciplined ambition driving toward a clear goal.

ar08.name: Strength
ar08.upright: Inner strength, courage, patience, compassion, self-control
ar08.reversed: Weakness, insecurity, self-doubt, raw emotion, lack of confidence
ar08.description: A woman gently holds open the jaws of a lion. Above her head floats the infinity symbol. She tames the beast not through force but through calm, loving strength. This card speaks to the power of patience, compassion, and gentle mastery over our primal instincts.

ar09.name: The Hermit
ar09.upright: Soul-searching, solitude, inner guidance, introspection, wisdom
ar09.reversed: Isolation, loneliness, withdrawal, reclusion, lost path
ar09.description: An old man stands alone on a mountain peak, holding a lantern that contains a six-pointed star. He has withdrawn from the world to seek truth within. The Hermit represents the wisdom found in solitude, the inner guide who shines a light through darkness for those willing to look inward.

ar10.name: Wheel of Fortune
ar10.upright: Luck, karma, life cycles, destiny, turning point, fate
ar10.reversed: Bad luck, resistance to change, breaking cycles, feeling stuck
ar10.description: A great wheel turns in the sky, inscribed with alchemical symbols. At its corners sit the four fixed signs of the zodiac: Taurus, Leo, Scorpio, Aquarius. The wheel symbolizes the eternal cycle of change — what rises must fall, and what falls will rise again.

ar11.name: Justice
ar11.upright: Fairness, truth, cause and effect, law, clarity, accountability
ar11.reversed: Dishonesty, unfairness, avoidance of responsibility, injustice
ar11.description: A crowned figure sits between two pillars, holding scales in one hand and a sword in the other. Justice represents cosmic balance — the law of cause and effect operating without sentiment. She asks us to act with integrity, knowing that our actions carry consequences.

ar12.name: The Hanged Man
ar12.upright: Surrender, letting go, suspension, new perspective, sacrifice
ar12.reversed: Stalling, martyrdom, indecision, resistance to necessary sacrifice
ar12.description: A man hangs upside-down from a living tree, his expression serene. One leg is bent, crossing the other to form a cross shape; his head glows with a halo. The Hanged Man has chosen to pause — to surrender control and see the world from a different angle. This stillness brings illumination.

ar13.name: Death
ar13.upright: Transformation, endings, change, transition, letting go of the past
ar13.reversed: Resistance to change, inability to move on, stagnation, fear of the unknown
ar13.description: Death rides a white horse, bearing a black banner with a white rose. Kings and commoners alike lie in his path. He does not bring annihilation but transformation — the clearing away of what has served its purpose so that new life can emerge. Death marks irreversible change.

ar14.name: Temperance
ar14.upright: Balance, moderation, patience, purpose, finding the middle path
ar14.reversed: Imbalance, excess, lack of long-term vision, missing the middle path
ar14.description: An angel with one foot on land and one in water pours liquid between two cups, blending them. Behind the figure, a path leads toward a golden crown in the distance. Temperance speaks to the art of balance — bringing together opposing forces to create something harmonious and sustainable.

ar15.name: The Devil
ar15.upright: Shadow self, addiction, materialism, bondage, unhealthy patterns
ar15.reversed: Release, breaking free, detachment, reclaiming power
ar15.description: A horned figure sits atop a pedestal to which two humans are chained — yet the chains are loose enough to remove. The Devil represents the unconscious patterns, addictions, and illusions that bind us. He is a mirror of our shadow: only by facing him can we free ourselves.

ar16.name: The Tower
ar16.upright: Upheaval, chaos, sudden change, revelation, unexpected disruption
ar16.reversed: Avoiding disaster, fear of change, delaying the inevitable
ar16.description: Lightning strikes a tall tower; two figures fall from its burning crown. The tower was built on a false foundation and must come down. The Tower represents sudden, unavoidable disruption — the kind that shatters illusions and forces a reckoning. Though devastating, it clears the way for truth.

ar17.name: The Star
ar17.upright: Hope, renewal, faith, serenity, inspiration, healing
ar17.reversed: Despair, hopelessness, loss of faith, disconnection
ar17.description: A nude woman kneels at the edge of a pool, pouring water onto the land and into the water simultaneously. Above her shine eight stars — one large, seven small. The Star follows the Tower as the calm after the storm, offering hope, renewal, and the assurance that healing is possible.

ar18.name: The Moon
ar18.upright: Illusion, fear, the unconscious, confusion, imagination, the unknown
ar18.reversed: Release of fear, clarity, repressed emotions surfacing, anxiety dissipating
ar18.description: A crayfish emerges from a pool beneath a full moon, which has both a bright and shadow face. A wolf and dog howl beneath it; a winding path disappears into the distance. The Moon represents the realm of the unconscious — where fears, dreams, and illusions intertwine and the familiar becomes strange.

ar19.name: The Sun
ar19.upright: Joy, success, vitality, confidence, clarity, positivity, abundance
ar19.reversed: Blocked happiness, overconfidence, inner child wounds, dimmed light
ar19.description: A child rides a white horse beneath a radiant sun, arms outstretched. Sunflowers bloom behind a garden wall. The Sun represents pure consciousness, joy, and the life force at its most exuberant. After the Moon's uncertainty, the Sun brings clear light, warmth, and the celebration of being alive.

ar20.name: Judgement
ar20.upright: Reflection, reckoning, awakening, absolution, heeding the call
ar20.reversed: Ignoring the call, self-doubt, refusing to learn from the past
ar20.description: An angel blows a trumpet above rising figures emerging from coffins. The figures raise their arms in answer to the call. Judgement represents a final reckoning — a moment of honest self-evaluation and awakening. It calls us to hear our higher calling and rise to meet it.

ar21.name: The World
ar21.upright: Completion, integration, achievement, wholeness, the journey's end
ar21.reversed: Incompleteness, stagnation, delayed success, lack of closure
ar21.description: A dancer wrapped in a purple sash moves within a laurel wreath. At the corners float the four fixed signs of the zodiac. The World represents the successful completion of a great cycle — full integration of experience, the merging of inner and outer, and the threshold of a new beginning.

cups01.name: Ace of Cups
cups01.upright: New love, intuition, creativity, emotional new beginning
cups01.reversed: Blocked emotions, unfulfilled feelings, emotional withdrawal
cups01.description: A great cup overflows with five streams of water, a dove descends bearing a sacred wafer. The Ace of Cups is the purest potential of the emotional realm — an offer of love, creative inspiration, and spiritual connection.

cups02.name: Two of Cups
cups02.upright: Partnership, unity, mutual attraction, soul connection
cups02.reversed: Broken communication, imbalance, tension in relationships
cups02.description: Two figures exchange cups beneath the caduceus of Hermes and a winged lion's head. The Two of Cups speaks to mutual recognition and the formation of a significant bond.

cups03.name: Three of Cups
cups03.description: Three women raise their cups in joyful celebration, surrounded by flowers and fruit. The Three of Cups honors the bonds of friendship and the power of shared joy.

cups04.name: Four of Cups
cups04.reversed: New openings, awakening from apathy, accepting an offer
cups04.description: A young man sits beneath a tree, arms crossed, while three cups stand before him and a mysterious hand offers a fourth. The Four of Cups invites reflection on what we may be overlooking.

cups05.name: Five of Cups
cups05.upright: Loss, grief, disappointment, focusing on what was lost
cups05.reversed: Moving forward, acceptance, finding what remains
cups05.description: A cloaked figure bows before three spilled cups, while two still stand behind. The Five of Cups confronts us with loss but reminds us that not everything is gone.

cups06.name: Six of Cups
cups06.upright: Nostalgia, childhood, innocence, generosity, happy memories
cups06.reversed: Stuck in the past, leaving home, naivety
cups06.description: A child offers a cup filled with flowers to a smaller figure. The Six of Cups carries the sweetness of memory and the grace of innocence.

cups07.name: Seven of Cups
cups07.upright: Choices, illusion, fantasy, wishful thinking, temptation
cups07.reversed: Clarity after confusion, choosing reality, alignment
cups07.description: Seven cups float in clouds, each holding a different vision. The Seven of Cups warns of the allure of fantasy and the challenge of too many options.

cups08.name: Eight of Cups
cups08.upright: Withdrawal, walking away, seeking something deeper, disillusionment
cups08.reversed: Fear of moving on, drifting, staying in an unfulfilling situation
cups08.description: A figure walks away from eight standing cups toward a mountain range under an eclipse. The Eight of Cups marks the moment we must leave behind what no longer fulfills us.

cups09.name: Nine of Cups
cups09.upright: Contentment, satisfaction, wish granted, emotional fulfillment
cups09.reversed: Greed, smugness, dissatisfaction beneath the surface
cups09.description: A man sits contentedly before nine cups arranged on a bench. Known as the 'wish card,' the Nine of Cups represents emotional satisfaction and getting what you desired.

cups10.name: Ten of Cups
cups10.upright: Harmony, happiness, family, fulfillment, divine love
cups10.description: A couple embraces as their children play before a rainbow of ten cups. The Ten of Cups is the fulfillment of the emotional journey — a life of love and harmony.

cups11.name: Page of Cups
cups11.upright: Creativity, intuition, new emotional messages, imaginative
cups11.description: A young figure holds a cup from which a fish peeks out. The Page of Cups brings messages of emotional opportunity and creative inspiration.

cups12.name: Knight of Cups
cups12.upright: Romance, charm, following the heart, idealism, offers
cups12.reversed: Unrealistic, moodiness, making promises you can't keep
cups12.description: A knight rides calmly on a white horse, holding a cup before him like a gift. The Knight of Cups follows his heart and brings romantic or creative offers.

cups13.name: Queen of Cups
cups13.upright: Compassion, nurturing, empathy, emotional security, intuition
cups13.description: A queen sits on a throne at the water's edge, holding an ornate cup. She is deeply intuitive, compassionate, and attuned to the emotional needs of those around her.

cups14.name: King of Cups
cups14.upright: Emotional balance, wisdom, calm, diplomatic, compassionate leadership
cups14.reversed: Manipulation, moodiness, emotional volatility beneath a calm exterior
cups14.description: A king sits on a throne floating amid turbulent seas, calm and composed. The King of Cups has mastered his emotional realm and leads with wisdom and heart.

pents01.name: Ace of Pentacles
pents01.upright: New financial opportunity, manifestation, abundance, new venture
pents01.reversed: Missed opportunity, poor financial planning, lack of foundation
pents01.description: A great hand emerges from a cloud holding a pentacle above a lush garden arch. The Ace of Pentacles offers a seed of material potential — a new beginning in the realm of work, health, or finance.

pents02.name: Two of Pentacles
pents02.upright: Balance, adaptability, time management, juggling priorities
pents02.description: A figure juggles two pentacles within an infinity loop while ships toss in waves behind. The Two of Pentacles speaks to the art of managing life's competing demands.

pents03.name: Three of Pentacles
pents03.reversed: Disharmony, lack of teamwork, poor planning
pents03.description: An artisan works on cathedral carvings as two planners consult a blueprint. The Three of Pentacles celebrates mastery developed through collaboration and dedication.

pents04.name: Four of Pentacles
pents04.reversed: Greed, materialism, fear of loss, hoarding
pents04.description: A figure clutches four pentacles to his body in a city behind him. The Four of Pentacles speaks to the drive for security, but warns of the price of holding too tightly.

pents05.name: Five of Pentacles
pents05.upright: Financial hardship, poverty, isolation, worry, difficult times
pents05.reversed: Recovery, spiritual poverty ending, acceptance of help
pents05.description: Two ragged figures trudge through snow past a stained-glass window glowing with light. The Five of Pentacles represents material hardship and spiritual isolation.

pents06.name: Six of Pentacles
pents06.upright: Generosity, charity, sharing wealth, giving and receiving
pents06.reversed: Debt, power imbalance, gifts with strings attached
pents06.description: A merchant holds scales in one hand and drops coins to kneeling beggars. The Six of Pentacles balances the energy of giving and receiving.

pents07.name: Seven of Pentacles
pents07.upright: Patience, long-term vision, investment, sustainable effort
pents07.reversed: Impatience, poor planning, limited reward for effort
pents07.description: A farmer leans on his hoe, surveying a vine laden with pentacles. The Seven of Pentacles invites a pause to assess the harvest of your efforts.

pents08.name: Eight of Pentacles
pents08.upright: Diligence, skill development, mastery, commitment, craftsmanship
pents08.reversed: Perfectionism, repetition without purpose, lack of ambition
pents08.description: An artisan carefully carves pentacles, row by row. The Eight of Pentacles is the card of the dedicated apprentice — skill built through patient, repetitive practice.

pents09.name: Nine of Pentacles
pents09.reversed: Financial setback, dependence, living beyond means
pents09.description: A woman strolls through a lush vineyard, a trained falcon on her gloved hand. The Nine of Pentacles celebrates hard-won material independence and the enjoyment of life's finer things.

pents10.name: Ten of Pentacles
pents10.upright: Legacy, family wealth, establishment, long-term security, permanence
pents10.reversed: Financial failure, instability, loss of legacy
pents10.description: An elder sits with dogs at his feet as family members gather around a grand archway. The Ten of Pentacles represents the culmination of material achievement — wealth, family, and lasting legacy.

pents11.name: Page of Pentacles
pents11.upright: Ambition, new skills, diligence, student, manifestation beginner
pents11.description: A young figure holds a pentacle before them, studying it carefully. The Page of Pentacles is the earnest student, beginning the journey of skill-building and material manifestation.

pents12.name: Knight of Pentacles
pents12.description: A knight on a heavy horse surveys a field, holding a pentacle. He moves slowly but never stops. The Knight of Pentacles embodies steady, reliable effort toward practical goals.

pents13.name: Queen of Pentacles
pents13.upright: Nurturing, practical, abundance, grounded, sensual, generous
pents13.reversed: Smothering, domestic neglect, insecurity about finances
pents13.description: A queen sits in a flowering arbor, cradling a pentacle. She is deeply connected to the natural world and generous with her material resources. She embodies practical wisdom and earthy abundance.

pents14.name: King of Pentacles
pents14.upright: Wealth, security, discipline, abundance, business leadership
pents14.description: A king sits on a throne decorated with bulls and grapes, surrounded by lush vegetation. The King of Pentacles has mastered the material world through discipline, patience, and wise stewardship.

swords01.name: Ace of Swords
swords01.upright: Clarity, breakthrough, truth, mental force, new idea
swords01.description: A hand emerges from clouds holding a gleaming sword crowned with a wreath. The Ace of Swords offers a cutting clarity — the power of the mind to perceive truth and cut through illusion.

swords02.name: Two of Swords
swords02.reversed: Indecision resolved, information revealed, confusion cleared
swords02.description: A blindfolded figure sits with crossed swords before a calm sea. The Two of Swords represents a temporary truce or deliberate avoidance — a moment when decisions are deferred.

swords03.name: Three of Swords
swords03.upright: Heartbreak, sorrow, grief, loss, painful truth
swords03.reversed: Recovery, forgiveness, releasing grief, moving past hurt
swords03.description: Three swords pierce a heart floating in stormy clouds. The Three of Swords does not soften the blow — it represents real pain, loss, and the grief that must be felt before healing can begin.

swords04.name: Four of Swords
swords04.reversed: Restlessness, recovery stalled, need for rest ignored
swords04.description: A knight lies in effigy on a stone tomb, one sword on the tomb, three on the wall above. The Four of Swords is a necessary pause — the restoration of body and mind after battle.

swords05.name: Five of Swords
swords05.upright: Conflict, defeat, tension, hollow victory, win at all costs
swords05.description: A figure collects swords while defeated opponents walk away in the background. The Five of Swords warns that victory achieved through ruthlessness may cost more than it gains.

swords06.name: Six of Swords
swords06.upright: Transition, moving on, calmer waters, escape, gradual recovery
swords06.reversed: Stuck in the past, unable to leave trouble behind
swords06.description: A ferryman poles a boat carrying a woman and child across dark water toward a brighter shore. Swords stand in the prow. The Six of Swords represents passage from difficulty toward something better.

swords07.name: Seven of Swords
swords07.upright: Deception, strategy, stealth, theft, getting away with something
swords07.description: A figure sneaks away from a military camp carrying five swords, leaving two behind. The Seven of Swords speaks to acts of cunning — whether strategic or deceptive, depending on context.

swords08.name: Eight of Swords
swords08.reversed: Release, new perspective, freedom from mental prison
swords08.description: A bound and blindfolded figure stands surrounded by eight swords. Yet the bindings are loose and the swords do not cage her — only her belief in her own powerlessness does. The Eight of Swords is a card of mental limitation.

swords09.name: Nine of Swords
swords09.upright: Anxiety, worry, nightmares, despair, mental anguish
swords09.reversed: Hope returning, fears exaggerated, inner turmoil lessening
swords09.description: A figure sits up in bed, face buried in hands, nine swords mounted on the wall behind. The Nine of Swords represents the 3 a.m. mind — the spiral of worry and dread that takes hold in the dark.

swords10.name: Ten of Swords
swords10.upright: Painful endings, betrayal, crisis, rock bottom, loss
swords10.reversed: Recovery, rising from ruin, resistance to inevitable endings
swords10.description: A figure lies face-down with ten swords in their back beneath a dark sky brightening at the horizon. The Ten of Swords marks total defeat — yet dawn is breaking, and what is over is truly over.

swords11.name: Page of Swords
swords11.upright: Curious, communicative, analytical, witty, new ideas
swords11.reversed: Gossipy, scattered, overly critical, all talk
swords11.description: A young figure stands on a hilltop, sword raised, scanning the horizon. The Page of Swords is quick-minded, eager to gather and share information.

swords12.name: Knight of Swords
swords12.upright: Decisive, ambitious, direct, fast-moving, driven by intellect
swords12.reversed: Impulsive, reckless, scattered, prone to conflict
swords12.description: A knight charges full-gallop across a windswept field. The Knight of Swords acts quickly and decisively, but risks missing the human cost of speed.

swords13.name: Queen of Swords
swords13.reversed: Cold, overly critical, bitter, cutting with words
swords13.description: A queen sits on a cloud-wreathed throne, one hand extended in welcome, the other raising a sword. She is sharp in mind and fair in judgment, unafraid of difficult truths.

swords14.name: King of Swords
swords14.upright: Intellectual authority, clear thinking, truth, structure, ethical leadership
swords14.reversed: Abuse of power, manipulation, cold-heartedness, tyranny
swords14.description: A king sits on a stone throne, sword raised, gazing directly forward. He rules by intellect and principle. The King of Swords demands clarity, integrity, and the courage to speak truth.

wands01.name: Ace of Wands
wands01.upright: Inspiration, new energy, creative spark, potential, passion
wands01.reversed: Blocked creativity, delays, lack of motivation, false starts
wands01.description: A hand emerges from a cloud holding a flowering wand. The Ace of Wands is pure creative fire — the initiating spark before any plan takes shape.

wands02.name: Two of Wands
wands02.upright: Future planning, decisions, discovery, long-term vision
wands02.reversed: Fear of unknown, staying safe, playing it small
wands02.description: A figure stands at a parapet, globe in hand, wands on either side. The Two of Wands shows someone who has achieved initial success and now turns their gaze toward what is possible next.

wands03.name: Three of Wands
wands03.upright: Expansion, foresight, enterprise, overseas ventures, awaiting results
wands03.description: A figure looks out over ships heading toward the horizon, three wands planted behind them. The Three of Wands shows plans in motion and the waiting that follows a bold launch.

wands04.name: Four of Wands
wands04.reversed: Conflict at home, lack of community, unstable foundation
wands04.description: Four wands are garlanded with flowers, two figures dancing beneath them. The Four of Wands marks a joyful arrival — a moment of celebration, community, and homecoming.

wands05.name: Five of Wands
wands05.upright: Conflict, competition, chaos, disagreement, many voices
wands05.reversed: Resolving conflict, avoiding confrontation, inner conflict
wands05.description: Five figures clash wands in apparent conflict. The Five of Wands represents the friction that arises when many energies compete — sometimes productive, sometimes simply chaotic.

wands06.name: Six of Wands
wands06.upright: Victory, recognition, success, confidence, public achievement
wands06.reversed: Egotism, fall from grace, lack of recognition, delayed success
wands06.description: A victorious rider is greeted by cheering crowds, wands raised in triumph. The Six of Wands celebrates earned recognition and the confidence that comes from genuine achievement.

wands07.name: Seven of Wands
wands07.upright: Perseverance, defensiveness, standing your ground, competition
wands07.reversed: Giving up, overwhelmed, capitulating to pressure
wands07.description: A figure holds a high ground position, defending against six wands below. The Seven of Wands embodies the determination to maintain what you've built in the face of challenge.

wands08.name: Eight of Wands
wands08.upright: Movement, speed, action, swift progress, travel
wands08.reversed: Delays, frustration, scattered energy, rushing without direction
wands08.description: Eight wands fly through clear air toward the earth. No figures appear — this is pure momentum. The Eight of Wands signals rapid movement and the swift resolution of situations.

wands09.name: Nine of Wands
wands09.upright: Resilience, courage, persistence, last stand, nearly there
wands09.reversed: Exhaustion, giving up near the finish, paranoia
wands09.description: A wounded but wary figure stands before eight upright wands, leaning on a ninth. The Nine of Wands reminds us that persistence in the final stretch is what separates success from failure.

wands10.name: Ten of Wands
wands10.upright: Burden, responsibility, stress, overextension, hard work
wands10.reversed: Releasing burdens, delegating, finding a lighter path
wands10.description: A figure struggles to carry ten wands toward a distant town. The Ten of Wands shows the weight of accumulated responsibilities — a call to reassess what is truly yours to carry.

wands11.name: Page of Wands
wands11.upright: Enthusiastic, adventurous, free spirit, creative, messenger of fire
wands11.reversed: Hasty, immature, scattered energy, unfocused passion
wands11.description: A young figure holds a staff, studying it with curiosity. The Page of Wands is the fire sign child — eager, inspired, and ready to explore.

wands12.name: Knight of Wands
wands12.description: A knight on a rearing horse charges forward, wand held aloft. The Knight of Wands is restless, charismatic, and driven by desire — he acts before he thinks.

wands13.name: Queen of Wands
wands13.upright: Confident, passionate, vibrant, independent, natural leadership
wands13.description: A queen sits on a sunflower throne, a black cat at her feet, wand in hand and sunflower in the other. The Queen of Wands radiates natural confidence and magnetic authority.

wands14.name: King of Wands
wands14.upright: Vision, entrepreneurship, leadership, inspiration, bold action
wands14.description: A king sits on a throne decorated with lions and salamanders, his wand blooming with life. The King of Wands is the visionary leader — passionate, creative, and driven to realize his vision at scale.

about.title: About Arké Tarot
about.appDescription: Arké Tarot is a free, privacy-first tool for tarot readings. Draw cards from a 78-card deck across multiple spread types, take notes, and export your reading as an image or a prompt for any AI assistant — no account required.
about.feature1: 78-card deck with upright and reversed meanings
about.feature2: Multiple spreads: Celtic Cross, Three-Card, Five-Card, Horseshoe, and Constellation
about.feature3: Export your spread as an image (PNG)
about.feature4: Generate an AI prompt for any assistant (Claude, ChatGPT, DeepSeek, and more)
about.feature5: Reading log stored locally in your browser
about.developer1: I'm Pedro, scientist, programmer, and matrix player.
about.developer2: I believe in the freedom of knowledge, and this is a great tool for that.
about.developer3: You can use it with your preferred AI agent, in paid or free versions.
about.developer4: No subscriptions or registrations needed — your information is yours and stays saved in your browser unless you decide to share it with an AI agent.
about.developer5: I created this app for my own enjoyment and share it with care, asking nothing in return.
about.developer6: But if you'd like to buy me a coffee as thanks, I'd gladly accept:
about.sourceText: The source code is open source and licensed under GPL-3.0. You can browse the code, report issues, or contribute on GitHub.
about.licenseCodeText: Licensed under the GNU General Public License v3.0 (GPL-3.0).
about.licenseImagesText: The tarot card images used in this app are sourced from Wikimedia Commons.
about.licenseContentText: All other original content in this app is licensed under Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International (CC BY-SA 4.0).

help.title: Help
help.howToUseIntro: Follow these steps to start a reading:
help.step1: Go to New Reading and choose a spread.
help.step2: Shuffle the deck, then draw cards one by one or all at once.
help.step3: For the Constellation spread, add cards freely and drag them into position.
help.step4: Write your reflections in the notes area below the spread.
help.step5: Save the reading to your Reading Log for future reference.
help.promptText: The "Copy Interpretation Prompt" button generates a text prompt with your spread and drawn cards. Paste it into any AI assistant — Claude, ChatGPT, DeepSeek, Gemini, or others — to get a detailed interpretation.
help.imageText: The "Copy as PNG" button copies the spread canvas as an image. Paste it into a chat, save it, or share it.
help.privacyText: All your data — readings, notes, and settings — is stored locally in your browser. Nothing is sent to any server. Your information stays on your device unless you actively copy a prompt and paste it into an AI assistant yourself.
help.installText: Arké Tarot can be installed on your phone, tablet, or desktop and used offline. Once installed it works without an internet connection — your readings, cards, and settings are always available. When you are online the app automatically loads the latest version.
help.installDesktop: On desktop browsers (Chrome, Edge): look for the install icon in the address bar, or open the browser menu and choose "Install Arké Tarot".
help.installMobile: On Android: tap the browser menu and choose "Add to Home Screen". On iPhone / iPad: tap the Share button in Safari then "Add to Home Screen".
help.installNote: AI interpretations happen outside the app — copy the prompt and paste it into any AI assistant of your choice, which requires an internet connection. Everything else — drawing cards, browsing the library, reading your log — works fully offline.
